steps.title: Make a table from the point and slope
steps.content: The point $(0,-8)$ is on the graph, so the $y$-intercept is $-8$. With slope $3$, moving one unit right raises $y$ by $3$, so another point is $(1,-5)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: In slope-intercept form, $f(x)=mx+b$. Here $m=3$ and $b=-8$, so $$f(x)=3x-8.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: All four choices with $3x$ have the right slope only if their constant term is correct. The distractors use intercepts $0$, $5$, and $11$. The graph has intercept $-8$, so B is correct.
